summary.deploymentMode: Catalyst Slate or Web Client Hosting for React, Catalyst Serverless Functions for API
summary.githubAutoFetch: Catalyst Pipelines can link this GitHub repository and trigger on push.
summary.ownerAction: Create/connect the Catalyst project, set pipeline variables, then push to the linked GitHub branch.

serviceMap.requiredService: Catalyst Slate or Web Client Hosting
serviceMap.prototypeEvidence: React + Vite command center with HashRouter. Repository build command emits the deployable app to dist.
functions/api contains the Node.js API handler, route contract, and smoke tests.

serviceMap.capability: API routing, throttling, and auth in front of Functions
serviceMap.prototypeEvidence: All backend capabilities are exposed under /api/* and can be fronted by Catalyst API Gateway after project binding.
Role-based demo login is implemented. Production login should replace demo credentials with Catalyst Authentication identities and roles.
data/import_instructions.md maps Users, PoliceStations, Cases, Accused, Relations, AuditLogs, and Reports tables.
Agent traces, chat transcripts, risk flags, and investigation session metadata are identified for NoSQL storage.
PDF briefs, evidence media, generated screenshots, and pipeline artifacts have a Stratus handoff path.
Hotspot, diffusion, graph, and similar-case outputs can be cached by district, crime type, and FIR ID.
The deterministic query engine demonstrates the workflow. Production RAG over FIR, legal, and SOP knowledge should use QuickML.

serviceMap.capability: OCR / Face / Text Analytics / Image Mod / Object Recognition / Barcode / ID Scanner
serviceMap.prototypeEvidence: Digital evidence and legal extraction screens define the intake points for Zia OCR, text analytics, ID scanning, and image recognition.
Browser speech input is available for demo reliability. Production STT, TTS, and translation should move to Zia Services.

serviceMap.capability: PDF / image-based report generation, screenshots, headless browser
serviceMap.prototypeEvidence: Investigation briefs export in-browser today. Scheduled/headless official report rendering should use SmartBrowz.

serviceMap.requiredService: Catalyst Cron or Catalyst Job Scheduling
serviceMap.prototypeEvidence: Nightly hotspot recomputation, stale-case alerts, and report cleanup jobs are identified.
Audit events can be emitted on data import, file upload, login, report generation, and supervisor approval.
Detective, Data, Network, Skeptic, and Report agent sequence is already modeled and ready for Circuits orchestration.
Supervisor report notifications, approval prompts, and escalation digests can be sent by Catalyst Mail.
catalyst-pipelines.yaml installs dependencies, validates the app, builds dist, runs API smoke tests, and deploys through Catalyst CLI.

githubFlow.name: Push repository to GitHub
githubFlow.detail: Keep .env files, .zcatalystrc, .catalyst, node_modules, and dist out of Git. Commit source, docs, seed data, catalyst.json, and catalyst-pipelines.yaml.

githubFlow.name: Connect Catalyst Pipelines to GitHub
githubFlow.detail: Create a Catalyst Pipeline, choose GitHub as the integration source, select the repository and branch, and commit the root catalyst-pipelines.yaml.

githubFlow.name: Set Catalyst pipeline variables
githubFlow.detail: Configure PROJECT_NAME, CATALYST_ORG, CATALYST_TOKEN, USER_NAME, and USER_PASSWORD in Catalyst Pipelines before first deploy.

githubFlow.name: Auto-build and deploy on push
githubFlow.detail: On every push to the linked branch, Catalyst fetches the repository, runs validation, builds the Vite app to dist, and executes catalyst deploy.

readinessGates: Synthetic data only; no real PII committed.
Root npm run build emits frontend output to dist.
API smoke tests cover chat, graph, hotspot, diffusion, patrol, report, Legal XAI, and Catalyst readiness.
Catalyst Authentication, API Gateway, Data Store, and Pipelines are explicitly mapped for production handoff.
Manual owner login/project binding remains the only non-code deployment step.
